seoTitle: Sinofuse (Zhongrong) Fuse Products | EV, Energy Storage, Solar Fuse Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Sinofuse high-voltage DC fuse products including EV fuses, energy storage fuses, and photovoltaic fuses. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What types of fuses does Sinofuse (Zhongrong) manufacture?
faqs.answer: Sinofuse specializes in high-voltage DC fuses for new energy applications: 1) EV Fuses - for electric vehicle battery packs, motors, and charging systems (500V-1000VDC); 2) Energy Storage Fuses - for battery energy storage systems and inverters (500V-1500VDC); 3) Photovoltaic Fuses - for solar inverters and combiner boxes (1000V-1500VDC); 4) Industrial Fuses - for general industrial power distribution and motor protection. All products feature high breaking capacity (up to 100kA) and comply with international standards including UL, IEC, and automotive requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right fuse for your EV, energy storage, or solar application.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for Sinofuse EV and energy storage fuses?
faqs.answer: Sinofuse offers comprehensive voltage ratings for DC applications: 1) 500VDC - for low-voltage EV and energy storage systems; 2) 750VDC - common for commercial EV and industrial applications; 3) 1000VDC - standard for many EV battery packs and solar systems; 4) 1500VDC - for utility-scale solar and large energy storage systems. Higher voltage ratings are available for specialized applications. All voltage ratings are DC working voltage with safety margins for transient conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage rating based on your system maximum DC voltage with appropriate safety margin. Contact our FAE for guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right current rating for high-voltage DC fuses?
faqs.answer: High-voltage DC fuse current selection: 1) Calculate maximum continuous current under worst-case conditions; 2) Apply 1.25x to 1.5x safety factor for steady loads; 3) Consider peak currents for EV acceleration or inverter startup; 4) Account for temperature derating - typically 20% at high ambient temperatures; 5) Verify coordination with other protection devices. Example: 200A continuous load requires 250A to 300A fuse rating. Always verify the selected fuse can protect against fault conditions and coordinate properly with system protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE with your system parameters for current rating recommendations and coordination studies.

faqs.question: What certifications do Sinofuse products carry?
faqs.answer: Sinofuse products hold comprehensive international certifications: 1) UL Recognition - for North American market compliance; 2) cULus - for Canada and US markets; 3) VDE - for European market; 4) TUV - German technical certification; 5) CCC - China Compulsory Certification; 6) IATF 16949 - automotive quality management; 7) ISO 9001 - quality management system; 8) ISO 14001 - environmental management. EV-specific products comply with UL 248-13 and IEC 60269-7 standards for automotive fuses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify required certifications for your target market. Contact our sales team for certification documentation.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for Sinofuse fuse products?
faqs.answer: Lead times vary by product type: 1) Standard EV fuses - 6-8 weeks; 2) Energy storage fuses - 8-10 weeks; 3) Photovoltaic fuses - 6-8 weeks; 4) Industrial fuses - 4-6 weeks; 5) Custom products - 10-12 weeks. BeiLuo maintains strategic inventory of popular ratings for faster delivery. Large volume orders may require production scheduling. Contact our sales team for current lead times and stock availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current stock status and lead times. Consider forecast agreements for high-volume requirements.

categories.name: EV Fuses
categories.description: Sinofuse EV fuses provide reliable high-voltage DC protection for electric vehicle battery packs, drive motors, onboard chargers, and DC/DC converters. These fuses are designed to handle high DC voltages (up to 1000VDC) and high breaking capacity (up to 100kA) required for EV applications. All EV fuses are manufactured under IATF 16949 automotive quality management systems and comply with UL 248-13 and IEC 60269-7 standards.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select EV Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting high-voltage DC fuses for electric vehicle applications based on voltage, current, and breaking capacity requirements.

faqs.question: What makes EV fuses different from standard automotive fuses?
faqs.answer: EV fuses differ from standard automotive fuses in several key aspects: 1) Voltage rating - EV fuses handle high DC voltages (500V-1000VDC) vs 32VDC for standard automotive; 2) Breaking capacity - EV fuses need 50kA to 100kA breaking capacity for battery short circuits vs 1kA to 10kA for standard; 3) Standards - EV fuses comply with UL 248-13 and IEC 60269-7 vs AEC-Q200 for standard; 4) Physical size - EV fuses are larger to handle higher power; 5) Time-current characteristics - optimized for semiconductor protection in power electronics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use EV-specific fuses for high-voltage DC circuits in electric vehicles. Standard automotive fuses are not suitable for EV battery systems.

faqs.question: How do I determine the breaking capacity requirement for EV battery fuses?
faqs.answer: Breaking capacity for EV battery fuses must exceed the maximum prospective short-circuit current: 1) Calculate battery short-circuit current based on cell chemistry and configuration; 2) Typical EV battery short-circuit currents range from 10kA to 100kA; 3) Select fuse with breaking capacity at least 1.25x calculated fault current; 4) Common ratings: 50kA for smaller packs, 80kA to 100kA for large battery packs; 5) Verify fuse can safely interrupt DC faults - DC is harder to interrupt than AC. Contact our FAE for fault current calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate battery short-circuit current and select fuse with adequate breaking capacity. Contact our FAE for assistance.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - EV Power Systems
faeReview.content: In my 10 years supporting EV power system designs, I've found the EVS series to be a reliable choice for medium-voltage EV applications. The 500V rating is ideal for commercial EV and some passenger vehicle battery packs. I particularly appreciate the consistent breaking performance across temperature ranges, which is crucial for EV safety. The bolt-on terminals provide excellent electrical contact and mechanical stability in high-vibration automotive environments. For sizing, I typically recommend 1.25x to 1.5x the maximum continuous current, considering both steady-state and peak loads during acceleration.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 500V protection with excellent breaking capacity for EV battery systems

faqs.question: What is the typical voltage drop across this fuse at rated current?
faqs.answer: The voltage drop across the EVS-500-200A at 200A rated current is typically less than 100mV. This low voltage drop minimizes power loss and heating in the fuse. At lower currents, the voltage drop is proportionally lower. The low resistance design helps maintain system efficiency in EV applications where every watt counts for range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider voltage drop when designing high-current EV systems. Contact our FAE for detailed resistance specifications.

faqs.question: How does this fuse perform under pre-charge conditions?
faqs.answer: The EVS-500-200A is designed to handle pre-charge currents typical in EV systems. During pre-charge, the fuse sees inrush current limited by the pre-charge resistor, typically 5-10A for a few seconds. The fuse's time-current characteristic allows this temporary overload without opening. For systems without pre-charge circuits, the fuse can handle capacitor inrush currents up to 10x rated current for brief periods (<10ms).
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement proper pre-charge circuits to limit inrush current. The fuse can handle typical pre-charge currents without issues.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High Voltage Systems
faeReview.content: The EVS-1000-400A represents the pinnacle of Sinofuse EV fuse technology. I've specified this fuse for several high-voltage EV platforms, and it consistently delivers exceptional performance. The 100kA breaking capacity is critical for large battery packs where fault currents can exceed 50kA. The 1000V rating accommodates the industry trend toward higher voltage architectures (800V+) for faster charging. I always emphasize proper torque on the M10 terminals - inadequate torque leads to heating and voltage drop. For installation, use calibrated torque wrenches and follow the specified 25-30 Nm range. The fuse's time-current curve is well-suited for semiconductor protection in modern EV power electronics.
faeReview.highlight: Ultimate protection for high-voltage EV systems with 100kA breaking capacity

alternativeParts.comparison: EVS-1000-400A=><EVS-1000-500A: Output current 500A > 400A (+25%), suitable for direct replacement

companionParts.description: 300A version for medium-current applications

faqs.question: What torque should be applied to the M10 mounting bolts?
faqs.answer: The recommended torque for M10 mounting bolts on the EVS-1000-400A is 25-30 Nm (18-22 ft-lb). Proper torque is critical for reliable electrical contact and to prevent overheating. Use a calibrated torque wrench and tighten in a star pattern if multiple bolts are present. Overtorquing can damage the fuse terminals, while undertorquing causes high contact resistance and heating. Always verify torque after initial installation and check periodically during maintenance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use calibrated torque wrench and apply 25-30 Nm torque. Verify torque after installation.

faqs.question: What applications benefit from 750V vs 500V or 1000V fuses?
faqs.answer: 750V fuses are optimal for: 1) Commercial EVs - buses and delivery vehicles balancing power and safety; 2) Medium-voltage platforms - many OEMs use 700-800V architectures; 3) Retrofit applications - upgrading from 500V without going to 1000V; 4) Cost-sensitive high-power applications - lower cost than 1000V fuses with adequate voltage margin. 750V provides good compromise between performance and cost for many commercial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 750V for commercial EVs and medium-voltage platforms. Use 1000V for premium EVs and 500V for entry-level vehicles.

faqs.question: When should I use 150A vs 200A for auxiliary circuits?
faqs.answer: Select 150A for: 1) DC/DC converters under 10kW; 2) Single PTC heaters under 5kW; 3) Small auxiliary loads with total <120A continuous. Select 200A for: 1) Larger DC/DC converters 10-15kW; 2) Multiple auxiliary loads; 3) Applications with high inrush currents. Always calculate actual maximum continuous current and apply 1.25x safety factor. Consider temperature derating at high ambient temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate maximum continuous current with 1.25x safety factor. Consider inrush and temperature derating.

categories.name: Energy Storage Fuses
categories.description: Sinofuse energy storage fuses provide critical protection for battery energy storage systems (BESS), inverters, and power conversion equipment. These fuses are designed for high-voltage DC applications up to 1500VDC with breaking capacities up to 100kA. Energy storage fuses feature time-delay characteristics to handle inrush currents from capacitors while providing fast protection during fault conditions.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Energy Storage Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting fuses for battery energy storage systems based on system voltage, battery capacity, and inverter requirements.

faqs.question: Why do energy storage systems need special fuses?
faqs.answer: Energy storage systems require specialized fuses because: 1) High DC voltages (500V-1500VDC) exceed standard AC fuse ratings; 2) Battery short-circuit currents can reach 50kA to 100kA, requiring high breaking capacity; 3) Inverter capacitors create inrush currents needing time-delay characteristics; 4) Continuous operation requires fuses rated for 100% duty cycle; 5) Safety standards like UL 9540 and IEC 62933 require specific protection. Standard fuses cannot safely interrupt high-voltage DC faults.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use dedicated energy storage fuses for all BESS applications. Contact our FAE for system-specific recommendations.

features: 1500VDC rating for utility-scale solar and storage

faeReview.author: Steven Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Energy Storage Systems
faeReview.content: I've worked on numerous utility-scale BESS projects, and the ESS-1500 series has become my go-to recommendation. The 1500V rating is essential for modern large-scale solar and storage installations where higher voltages reduce DC cabling costs. The time-delay characteristic is crucial - I've seen standard fast-acting fuses nuisance-blow during inverter startup when capacitors charge. The 100kA breaking capacity handles the massive fault currents from large lithium-ion battery racks. One important consideration is coordination with DC breakers - the fuse should act as backup protection for faults beyond the breaker's capacity. Always perform a coordination study for large systems.
faeReview.highlight: Ideal for utility-scale BESS with 1500V rating and time-delay for inverter protection

faqs.question: How do I coordinate this fuse with DC breakers in BESS applications?
faqs.answer: Fuse and DC breaker coordination in BESS: 1) The DC breaker handles normal overloads and switching; 2) The fuse provides backup protection for high fault currents beyond breaker rating; 3) Breaker instantaneous trip should be below fuse melting current for overloads; 4) Fuse should operate faster than breaker for short-circuits above breaker capacity; 5) Typical coordination: 250A fuse with 200A breaker provides good separation. Perform time-current curve analysis to verify coordination. The fuse acts as ultimate protection if breaker fails or for catastrophic faults.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size breaker at 80% of fuse rating. Perform coordination study with actual curves.

faqs.question: What size BESS is the ESS-1000-200A suitable for?
faqs.answer: The ESS-1000-200A is suitable for: 1) Commercial BESS: 50-200kWh with 1000V battery voltage and 100-150kW inverters; 2) Large residential: 20-50kWh with high-power inverters; 3) Industrial peak shaving: Medium-power applications. Calculate based on: Max current = Inverter power / Battery voltage x 1.25 safety factor. For 100kW inverter at 1000V: 100A x 1.25 = 125A, so 200A fuse provides good margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on inverter power and battery voltage. Use 1.25x safety factor.

faqs.question: When do I need 400A vs 250A for utility-scale BESS?
faqs.answer: Select 400A for: 1) Inverters >500kW; 2) Battery racks >2MWh; 3) Main DC bus protection; 4) Systems with parallel battery strings. Select 250A for: 1) Inverters 250-500kW; 2) Individual battery rack protection; 3) Branch circuit protection. Calculate: Max current = Total inverter power / DC voltage x 1.25. For 1MW at 1500V: 667A, so use multiple 400A fuses or higher rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total system current with 1.25x margin. Use multiple fuses for very large systems.

faqs.question: Is 125A sufficient for typical home battery systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, 125A is sufficient for most residential battery systems: 1) Typical home batteries: 10-20kWh with 5-10kW inverters; 2) At 400V battery voltage, 10kW = 25A; 3) At 1000V, 10kW = 10A; 4) With 1.25x safety factor: 31A at 400V or 12.5A at 1000V; 5) 125A provides 4x margin at 400V or 10x at 1000V. Even for large 20kW systems, 125A provides adequate margin. Only very large homes with multiple battery units need higher ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: 125A sufficient for typical residential systems up to 20kW. Calculate based on actual inverter power.

categories.name: Photovoltaic Fuses
categories.description: Sinofuse photovoltaic fuses provide reliable overcurrent protection for solar power systems including inverters, combiner boxes, and DC distribution. These fuses are specifically designed for high-voltage DC solar applications up to 1500VDC with ratings suitable for both residential and utility-scale installations. PV fuses feature gPV classification per IEC 60269-6 for photovoltaic applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select PV Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting fuses for photovoltaic systems based on string current, inverter voltage, and array configuration.

faqs.question: What is gPV classification for PV fuses?
faqs.answer: gPV (general Purpose Photovoltaic) is a fuse classification per IEC 60269-6 specifically for photovoltaic applications. gPV fuses: 1) Are tested for DC operation up to 1500V; 2) Can safely interrupt low overcurrents typical of PV fault conditions; 3) Are designed for outdoor temperature extremes; 4) Have full-range protection (operate at all overcurrents). Standard gG or gL fuses are not suitable for PV because they may not interrupt low DC fault currents reliably. Always use gPV-classified fuses for solar applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always specify gPV-classified fuses for photovoltaic applications. Verify markings on fuse body.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Solar Power Systems
faeReview.content: I've specified PV fuses for hundreds of solar installations, and the PV-1500 series consistently delivers reliable performance. The gPV classification is essential - I've seen installations where standard fuses failed to clear faults, creating fire hazards. The 1500V rating supports the industry trend toward higher voltage systems that reduce DC wiring costs. For string sizing, I typically recommend fuses at 1.5x to 1.8x the string short-circuit current (not operating current). For example, a string with 10A Isc gets a 15A or 20A fuse. The 30A rating is perfect for larger strings or parallel configurations. Always use proper PV fuse holders - standard industrial holders may not be rated for 1500VDC.
faeReview.highlight: gPV-certified protection for 1500V solar systems with reliable fault clearing

1500VDC rated fuse holder for PV applications

faqs.question: How do I size PV fuses for solar strings?
faqs.answer: PV fuse sizing formula: Fuse Rating = 1.5 x to 1.8 x Isc (string short-circuit current). Example: String with 10A Isc requires 15A to 18A fuse, so select 15A or 20A standard rating. Never size based on operating current (Imp) - always use Isc. Consider: 1) Module Isc at Standard Test Conditions (STC); 2) Temperature coefficient - Isc increases at low temperatures; 3) Future degradation - modules produce more current when new; 4) Safety margin - NEC requires minimum 1.56x Isc. Round up to next standard fuse size.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate 1.5x to 1.8x string Isc and select next standard rating. Contact our FAE for complex array designs.

faqs.question: When should I use 20A vs 15A for PV strings?
faqs.answer: Use 20A for: 1) Standard strings with 10-13A Isc; 2) Systems in moderate climates; 3) Cost-optimized designs. Use 15A for: 1) Smaller strings with 8-10A Isc; 2) High-temperature installations where derating needed; 3) Conservative designs with extra safety margin. Calculate: Fuse = 1.5-1.8 x Isc. For 10A Isc: 15-18A, so 20A is appropriate. For 8A Isc: 12-14.4A, so 15A is better choice.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate 1.5-1.8x Isc. Use 20A for standard strings, 15A for smaller strings or high-temperature.

products.name: 1000V 15A PV Fuse
products.shortDescription: 1000VDC 15A gPV fuse for legacy solar systems and cost-sensitive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The PV-1000-15A is a cost-effective gPV fuse for 1000VDC solar systems.
This fuse is ideal for legacy installations and cost-sensitive residential projects where 1500V is not required.
The 15A rating handles typical residential strings while providing full gPV protection.
features: Full-range protection for all fault currents

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Solar Power Systems
faeReview.content: The PV-1000-15A provides a cost-effective option for 1000V systems. While the industry is moving to 1500V, many existing systems and budget-conscious installations still use 1000V. This fuse offers the same gPV protection at a lower cost point. The 10x38mm size is widely available and fits many existing fuse holders. I recommend this for retrofit projects and cost-sensitive residential installations where 1500V is not required.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 1000V/15A PV fuse for legacy and budget-conscious installations

alternativeParts.comparison: PV-1000-15A=><PV-1500-20A: Output current 20A > 15A (+33%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher voltage and current for modern systems

companionParts.description: 10A version for small strings
1000VDC rated fuse holder

faqs.question: Should I use 1000V or 1500V fuses for new installations?
faqs.answer: For new installations, we recommend 1500V fuses even if your current system is 1000V: 1) Future-proofing - system voltage may be upgraded later; 2) Industry trend - 1500V is becoming standard; 3) Minimal cost difference - 1500V fuses only slightly more expensive; 4) Availability - 1500V products have better long-term availability. Use 1000V fuses for: 1) Legacy system maintenance; 2) Very cost-sensitive projects; 3) Replacement of existing 1000V fuses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1500V for new installations. Use 1000V for legacy systems and retrofits.

products.name: 1500V 25A PV Fuse
products.shortDescription: 1500VDC 25A gPV fuse for large strings and high-power solar modules.
descriptionParagraphs: The PV-1500-25A is designed for large solar strings and high-power modules with higher short-circuit currents.
With 1500VDC rating and 25A current, this fuse handles modern high-wattage solar modules in large strings.
The gPV classification ensures reliable protection for all fault conditions in high-power arrays.
features: 25A rating for large strings and high-power modules
Full-range protection for all fault currents
High temperature rating for rooftop installations

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Solar Power Systems
faeReview.content: The PV-1500-25A is essential for modern high-power solar installations. With modules now exceeding 500W, short-circuit currents are higher than ever. A typical string of 500W+ modules can have 15-18A Isc, requiring 25-30A fuses. The 25A rating provides good margin for these high-power strings while the 1500V rating supports modern system architectures. I've used this fuse for utility-scale projects with the latest high-wattage modules with excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 1500V/25A PV fuse for large strings and high-power modules

alternativeParts.comparison: PV-1500-25A=><PV-1500-30A: Output current 30A > 25A (+20%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for very large strings or multiple parallel strings

companionParts.description: 20A version for standard strings
1500VDC rated fuse holder for PV applications

faqs.question: When do I need 25A vs 20A for PV strings?
faqs.answer: Use 25A for: 1) High-wattage modules (>450W) with Isc >13A; 2) Large strings with 14-16A Isc; 3) Parallel string configurations; 4) Systems in cold climates where Isc is higher. Use 20A for: 1) Standard modules (300-450W) with Isc 10-13A; 2) Typical residential strings; 3) Moderate climate installations. Calculate: Fuse = 1.5-1.8 x Isc. For 14A Isc: 21-25A, so 25A is appropriate. Always check module datasheet for actual Isc at STC.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate 1.5-1.8x Isc. Use 25A for high-power modules and large strings.

categories.name: Industrial Fuses
categories.description: Sinofuse industrial fuses provide reliable overcurrent protection for general industrial applications including power distribution, motor protection, and control systems. These fuses cover AC and DC industrial applications with voltage ratings up to 1000V and current ratings from tens to thousands of amps. Industrial fuses comply with IEC 60269 and UL 248 standards.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Industrial Fuses
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting industrial fuses based on load type, voltage, and coordination requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between gG and aM fuse types?
faqs.answer: gG (general purpose) and aM (motor protection) are IEC fuse classes: gG fuses provide full-range protection against overloads and short-circuits, suitable for general circuits. aM fuses provide short-circuit protection only (must be used with overload relays), designed specifically for motor circuits. gG fuses operate at lower overcurrents (typically 1.6x rating), while aM fuses allow motor starting currents (operate at 4x to 8x rating). For motors, aM fuses prevent nuisance blowing during startup while providing short-circuit protection. For general circuits, gG fuses provide complete protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use gG for general circuits, aM for motor circuits with separate overload protection.

products.name: 690V 160A Industrial Fuse
products.shortDescription: 690V AC/DC 160A gG fuse for industrial power distribution and motor protection. Ideal for industrial and consumer applications with excellent performance and reliability.
descriptionParagraphs: The IND-690-160A is a versatile industrial fuse for power distribution and motor protection.
With 690V rating, this fuse covers standard industrial voltage levels.
The gG characteristic provides full-range protection for general circuits.
features: High breaking capacity 100kA for fault protection
NH size for standard industrial mounting
Visual indicator for blown fuse detection

faeReview.author: Robert Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Systems
faeReview.content: The IND series provides cost-effective protection for industrial applications. The 690V rating covers most industrial voltage levels (380V, 400V, 480V, 575V, 600V). I typically recommend gG fuses for general circuits and aM for motor circuits. The NH mounting system is industry standard, making installation straightforward. For motor applications, remember that aM fuses only provide short-circuit protection - you still need overload relays. One common mistake is using gG fuses for motors, causing nuisance blowing during startup. The visual indicator is helpful for maintenance - you can see if a fuse has operated without removing it. For critical applications, consider fuse monitoring relays to provide remote indication.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable industrial protection with standard NH mounting for easy installation

alternativeParts.comparison: IND-690-160A=><IND-690-200A: Output current 200A > 160A (+25%), suitable for direct replacement

companionParts.description: 125A version for smaller loads
NH1 fuse base for panel mounting

faqs.question: Can this fuse be used for both AC and DC applications?
faqs.answer: The IND-690-160A is rated for 690V AC and 500V DC. For AC applications, the full 690V rating applies. For DC applications, the rating is limited to 500V DC due to the difficulty of interrupting DC arcs. The breaking capacity is also different: 100kA for AC, typically 50kA for DC (verify datasheet for specific DC breaking capacity). Always check the voltage and breaking capacity ratings for your specific application. For DC applications above 500V, use dedicated DC fuses from our EV or energy storage series.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for AC up to 690V or DC up to 500V. For higher DC voltages, use dedicated DC fuse series.

The NH1 size is widely available and fits standard industrial fuse bases and switch disconnectors.
features: 690V AC rating for industrial power systems

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: The IND-690-100A is a workhorse fuse for industrial applications. The 100A rating is perfect for motors up to 50kW and the gG characteristic provides complete protection. I specify this fuse for general industrial control panels and motor protection applications. The NH1 size is universally available and the price point is competitive. The dual AC/DC rating adds flexibility for applications with both power types.
faeReview.highlight: Versatile 100A industrial fuse for motor and distribution protection

alternativeParts.comparison: IND-690-100A=><IND-690-125A: Output current 125A > 100A (+25%), suitable for direct replacement

companionParts.description: 80A version for smaller motors
NH1 fuse base for panel mounting

faqs.question: What size motor can be protected with 100A fuse?
faqs.answer: Motor sizing for gG fuses: 1) Calculate motor full load current (FLC) from kW rating and voltage; 2) Apply 1.6-2.0x multiplier for starting current; 3) Select fuse rating above this value. Example: 37kW motor at 400V has ~70A FLC. Starting current 5-7x = 350-490A. gG fuse operates at 1.6x in 1 hour, so 100A fuse allows 160A continuously - may nuisance blow. Better to use 125A or aM fuse for motor protection. For general distribution, 100A protects circuits up to 100A continuous load.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use aM fuses for motor protection. Use gG for distribution and general circuits.

products.shortDescription: 500V AC/DC 250A gG industrial fuse for high-current distribution and motor protection.
descriptionParagraphs: The IND-500-250A is a high-current industrial fuse designed for demanding power distribution and large motor protection applications.
With 250A rating and NH2 size, this fuse handles high-power industrial loads while providing reliable protection.
The 500V rating covers standard industrial voltages up to 480V systems.
features: Suitable for large motors and distribution

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: The IND-500-250A handles the high currents needed for large industrial motors and distribution. The NH2 size provides the thermal mass needed for 250A continuous operation. I use this fuse for main distribution panels and large motor protection (110kW+). The 500V rating covers standard 480V industrial systems. The equal AC/DC rating is useful for applications with both power types. The 100kA breaking capacity handles high fault currents in industrial environments.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 250A fuse for large motors and main distribution

alternativeParts.comparison: IND-500-250A=><IND-690-250A: Output current 250A = 250A (same), Voltage 690V > 500V (higher), suitable for direct replacement

companionParts.description: 200A version for smaller loads
NH2 fuse base for panel mounting

faqs.question: When should I use NH2 vs NH1 size fuses?
faqs.answer: NH size selection: NH00 (00) - up to 100A, compact size; NH0 (0) - up to 160A; NH1 (1) - up to 250A, most common industrial size; NH2 (2) - up to 400A; NH3 (3) - up to 630A. Select based on current rating and physical space. NH1 is most common and cost-effective for 100-250A. NH2 required for >250A. Consider: 1) Current requirement; 2) Available space; 3) Fuse base compatibility; 4) Cost - larger sizes cost more.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NH1 for 100-250A (most common). Use NH2 for >250A. Match fuse base to fuse size.

faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Control Systems
faeReview.content: The IND-500-63A is perfect for control panels and small motor applications. The NH00 size is compact and fits in crowded control panels. The 63A rating handles most control circuits and small motors up to 30kW. I specify this fuse for PLC panels, machine tools, and small motor control centers. The price is very competitive for the NH00 size. The dual AC/DC rating is useful for control systems with both power types.

companionParts.description: 50A version for control circuits
NH00 fuse base for panel mounting

faqs.question: What is the difference between gG and aM fuse types?
faqs.answer: gG (general purpose) fuses provide full-range protection against overloads and short-circuits. They operate at relatively low overcurrents (typically 1.6x rating in 1 hour). aM (motor protection) fuses provide short-circuit protection only and must be used with overload relays. They allow high motor starting currents (operate at 4-8x rating). For motors: aM prevents nuisance blowing during startup while overload relay handles overload protection. For general circuits: gG provides complete protection. IND-500-63A is gG type suitable for general circuits and small motors with appropriate sizing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use gG for general circuits. Use aM for motor circuits with separate overload protection.
